requirements.description: Every predictive-operations child bead maps to pushed commits, source paths, tests or fixtures, evidence artifacts, validation commands, close reason, and claim-boundary text.

The checklist covers predictive telemetry fusion, validation scheduling, semantic compaction quality, extension hostcall cost attribution, operator-perceived latency, redundant-agent-work detection, source boundaries, pushed refs, and quality gates.
A passing gate may close the final gate and parent epic only after the closeout commit lands and the branch is pushed. A failing gate must emit follow-up Beads instead of optimistic closeout.

The gate must record that it does not mutate Agent Mail, RCH workers, Beads outside the normal closeout flow, runtime state, installed extensions, temp artifacts, source files, or production services.
The gate must record Python compilation, script self-test, JSON validation, git diff cleanliness, staged UBS posture, and Beads ledger reconciliation.
The closeout gate is evidence over Beads, git, docs, tests, generated target/perf outputs, RCH, Agent Mail health, UBS, and child artifacts. It does not replace those sources of truth.
The closeout gate must not make release-facing performance, capacity, benchmark, strict drop-in, or live-swarm safety claims.
The predictive-operations gate may reference sixth- and seventh-wave authority boundaries but must not rewrite those contracts or evidence.
